comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
@edsantiago Looks like you registry script does not work on ubuntu?

Entirely my fault: I took shortcuts when writing the script, and deferred error handling "for later". I deferred too long. I apologize.

I've filed #6375 to address this; errors should now be visible to the caller. @rhatdan if that passes, please feel free to rebase onto it so we can see what's failing here.
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably #6359) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
@edsantiago It looks like podman does not like the `--root` flag on Ubuntu. I have no idea what is causing this?

````
../podman-registry: line 102: --root: command not found
````
It looks like `$PODMAN` is not being set. It should be set in line 17 via:
````bash
PODMAN=${PODMAN:-$(type -p podman)}
````
I'm leaving home now and can't check, but I have a vague memory that there's some code that installs podman, then deletes all the installed files. I don't know why that would happen only on Ubuntu and not Fedora though. 

My two suggestions are to (1) set `PODMAN=/path/to/podman` in whatever code invokes this script, or (2) change the default in the script to `$(dirname $0)/../bin/podman` (to use the podman built in the tree). I don't have a good sense for which is better or if there are other options.

Gotta go, sorry. HTH

Found it: [contrib/cirrus/lib.sh:remove_packaged_podman_files()](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/contrib/cirrus/lib.sh#L403). What I don't understand is how then the script would work on Fedora.

I'm leaning slightly toward this solution, if you'd like to apply it and test with it.
```diff
--- a/hack/podman-registry
+++ b/hack/podman-registry
@@ -14,7 +14,7 @@ PODMAN_REGISTRY_PASS=
 PODMAN_REGISTRY_PORT=

 # Podman binary to run
-PODMAN=${PODMAN:-$(type -p podman)}
+PODMAN=${PODMAN:-$(dirname $0)/../bin/podman}

 # END   defaults
```
@rhatdan it's not going to work; see #6390. Sorry.
Yikes! Could this be a problem with a bad golang version on ubuntu?
@AkihiroSuda @giuseppe Looks like rootless kit is pulling in requirements for newer versions of golang for ubuntu versions.  What do you guys suggest we do?
Could you be more specific? It should work with Go >= 1.13 and probably also 1.12.

When compiling the code on Ubuntu we are seeing these errors.
```
[+0124s] # github.com/containers/libpod/vendor/k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/errors
[+0124s] vendor/k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/errors/errors.go:99:10: undefined: errors.Is
[+0124s] # github.com/containers/libpod/vendor/github.com/rootless-containers/rootlesskit/pkg/port/builtin/parent
[+0124s] vendor/github.com/rootless-containers/rootlesskit/pkg/port/builtin/parent/parent.go:94:9: undefined: "github.com/containers/libpod/vendor/github.com/pkg/errors".Is
```

Bumping up https://github.com/pkg/errors works?

https://github.com/pkg/errors seems tested with Go >= 1.11: https://github.com/pkg/errors/blob/614d223910a179a466c1767a985424175c39b465/.travis.yml#L4
Ok so we probably need to update the VM images.
